research_context.market: Hong Kong product teams evaluating AI user or synthetic user research tools
research_context.purpose: Generate a reusable V5 persona who is genuinely relevant to UX, product design, and design-research-adjacent evaluation without pre-encoding a synthetic-user conclusion.
research_context.platform_rule: Build a person first. UX context may include stable role facts, research exposure, and prototype habits, but must not encode whether the person will endorse or reject AI users.
allowed_domain_context_examples: artifact comfort such as Figma or journey maps
career mix of agency and in-house work
forbidden_core_shortcuts: main pain is not enough interview transcripts
already believes empathy can be automated
thinks synthetic users are fake by default
would definitely use this for all testing
would definitely reject this for all testing
is only here to defend human research

fixed.occupation: Senior UX designer with regular involvement in user interviews, prototyping, and product-shaping work

preferred.persona_generation_rule: Generate the person from ordinary life, design practice, work context, and human difference axes first. UX context should remain stable and reusable.
preferred.sample_eligibility_rule: The person should plausibly participate in user interviews, synthesis, prototyping, and design critique, and should care about the difference between shallow answers and behaviorally useful evidence.
preferred.product_context_rule: Use UX context only for stable role facts such as interview exposure, prototype habits, empathy style, and collaboration boundaries. Do not encode a fixed verdict on AI research.
preferred.career_path_expectation: Should include believable movement through design craft, interface or service work, and regular contact with research or usability feedback.
variation_targets: high empathy language versus dry operational critique

human_difference_axes_expectations.control_preference: Should want to inspect how a synthesized persona or conclusion was formed before trusting it.
human_difference_axes_expectations.trust_style: Should care about behavioral plausibility, nuance retention, and whether edge cases are flattened away.
human_difference_axes_expectations.complexity_tolerance: Can be medium to high if complexity helps preserve nuance.
human_difference_axes_expectations.decision_tempo: Should vary between exploratory openness and sharper critique once a concept becomes concrete.
human_difference_axes_expectations.relationship_to_money: Money does not need to dominate; time, craft integrity, and credibility may matter more.
human_difference_axes_expectations.need_for_explanation: Should want concrete examples, not only claims.
human_difference_axes_expectations.guidance_preference: May prefer tools that support critique and comparison rather than automate judgment entirely.

negative_prompting: Do not make the UX designer a stereotype of empathy slogans.
Do not assume formal deep research specialization unless the story supports it.
Do not turn the person into a pure anti-AI gatekeeper.
Do not overfit the persona to one methodology.
